channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Witkoff and Kushner to Travel to Pakistan for Talks on Iran
item.description: Witkoff and Kushner will travel to Pakistan for talks on Iran. Witkoff and Kushner to Travel to Pakistan for Talks on Iran.

item.title: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
item.description: Seven people died in a Russian attack on Ukraine. Dnipro was hardest hit. Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine.

item.title: US envoys head to Pakistan for Iran war talks
item.description: US envoys are heading to Pakistan for Iran war talks. The US is "looking for a face-saving way to escape the war quagmire it has become trapped in", says Iran's defence ministry.

item.title: 850 Of Elon Musk's Recent X Posts On Discrimination Against White People
item.description: Elon Musk posted 850 times on X about discrimination against white people. Musk's focus on race has also drawn criticism from some of his former supporters. 850 Of Elon Musk's Recent X Posts On Discrimination Against White People.

item.title: Bangladesh issues nationwide security alert over possible militant attacks
item.description: Bangladesh issued a nationwide security alert due to possible militant attacks. "The alert has been issued based on intelligence reports,” a police headquarters (PHQ) official said, requesting anonymity. The official described the alert as “urgent and confidential”.

item.title: No Degree? No Platform. China Fines Fake Finance Gurus $14,000 And Deletes Their Accounts
item.description: China fined fake finance gurus $14,000 and deleted their social media accounts after new influencer rules took effect, triggering severe consequences across the digital economy. China changed influencer rules, triggering severe consequences across digital economy.

item.title: The 85-Year-Old Widow Snagged by Trump’s Immigration Crackdown
item.description: Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, an 85-year-old French widow of a former G. I., was deported from the US under Trump's immigration crackdown. In her first interview since being deported, she recounted her experience in ICE detention.

item.title: Gunmen stage simultaneous attacks across Mali, army says
item.description: Gunmen staged simultaneous attacks across Mali, targeting the capital Bamako and several other locations in an apparently coordinated assault involving multiple groups. Capital Bamako and several other locations hit in an apparently coordinated ⁠assault involving multiple groups.

item.title: 2nd Round Of Talks In Pakistan Today, Commercial Flights Resume In Tehran
item.description: Commercial flights resumed in Tehran as US-Iran tensions eased, coinciding with a second round of talks between the two nations. US-Iran War News Live Update:. 2nd Round Of Talks In Pakistan Today, Commercial Flights Resume In Tehran.

item.title: Europe Prepares for a Longer War in Ukraine, With No Strategy to End It
item.description: Europe prepares for a longer war in Ukraine with no clear path to victory or peace. With American dealmakers wrapped up with Iran, neither Russia nor Ukraine has a clear path to victory.

item.title: US, Iran To Hold 2nd Round Of Talks In Pakistan Today. What's On Agenda?
item.description: Iran and the US will hold a second round of peace talks in Pakistan. The previous round occurred on April 11-12, amid a delicate ceasefire. The previous round of talks was held on April 11-12.

item.title: ‘Astonishing’ discovery could help save children from deadly disfiguring condition
item.description: Researchers found a new bacteria species in noma patients that could lead to better treatments.

item.title: Top Iranian and Pakistani officials meet in Islamabad
item.description: Iran's FM Abbas Araghchi met Pakistani officials in Islamabad for talks. Iran’s FM Abbas Araghchi is in Islamabad for talks with Pakistani officials, as US envoys are also set to arrive.

item.title: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
item.description: Palestinians in the West Bank and one Gazan city voted in local elections, with Hamas and other groups boycotting the process. Local elections are held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

item.title: Iran executes man over 'mission' for Israel's spy agency
item.description: Iran executed Erfan Kiani for allegedly working with Israel's spy agency. Erfan Kiani was hanged after his sentence was upheld by the country's Supreme Court, the judiciary's Mizan Online website said.

item.title: Iran Foreign Minister Araghchi meets Pakistan Army chief Munir
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi met with Pakistan's Army chief Munir, coinciding with the resumption of commercial flights from Tehran's international airport after a two-month hiatus. Iran resumes commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for the first time since the conflict with the U. S.

item.title: Overnight Russian attacks on Ukraine kill five, injure 30
item.description: Russia launched over 600 drones and 47 missiles at Ukraine, killing five and injuring 30 in eight regions. Russia launched more than 600 drones and 47 missiles as it targeted eight regions in Dnipro, authorities say.

item.title: Iran resumes commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for first time since conflict
item.description: Iran resumed commercial flights from Tehran's international airport after a two-month halt due to conflict. Iran has resumed commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for the first time since the conflict with the U. S.

item.title: On Iran’s Border, Cooking Oil Trade Is a Snapshot of the Country’s Struggles
item.description: Iran's economic crisis has forced many to cross into Turkey to buy basic goods like cooking oil. The war has exacerbated Iran’s economic crisis, forcing many to cross the border into Turkey to buy the most basic goods.

item.title: Ireland to demand Israel pay for settler demolition of Palestinian school
item.description: Ireland will demand Israel repay the cost of a demolished Palestinian school. Ireland will demand that Israeli authorities repay the cost of a school that was demolished by settlers. Ireland to demand Israel pay for settler demolition of Palestinian school.
